name: the-guns-of-august
description: Barbara W. Tuchman's The Guns of August — a narrative history of the first month of WWI, tracing the catastrophic miscalculations, rigid war plans, and the Battle of the Marne that determined the course of the 20th century.
useCase: Understand the first month of WWI — the Schlieffen Plan, the Battle of the Marne, and how inflexible plans and failed assumptions led to catastrophe.
knownRisks: Tuchman's narrative style prioritizes storytelling over analytical history — some details are dramatized
The book focuses almost exclusively on Western leadership — minimal coverage of ordinary soldiers or non-European perspectives
Written in 1962 — some interpretations have been updated by subsequent scholarship
